result:
If I could change one thing, I'd want every person to truly feel - not just intellectually know, but viscerally experience - how interconnected we all are. 

Not in some abstract way, but that bone-deep recognition that the stranger on the street, the person across the world, even those we disagree with most strongly, are all part of the same vast human story. That their joys and sorrows are as real as our own.

I think so much cruelty comes from our ability to forget this - to make others into abstractions or enemies rather than fellow travelers. If we couldn't help but feel that connection, maybe we'd find it harder to harm each other, easier to extend patience and understanding.

What change would you make?

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
